exampleSentences: Món phở ở quán này siêu ngon, anh muốn ăn thêm nữa.
Chị vừa mua một cái điện thoại siêu mới và rất đẹp.
Em thấy bài kiểm tra hôm nay siêu khó, phải học nhiều hơn.
notes_md:
**siêu** = super, extremely. It makes something sound very impressive or intense.

It’s usually placed **before adjectives** to boost them, stronger and more emotional than *rất*.  

It’s informal and expressive, common in speech and online. In fixed words, it can also mean “super-” like in *siêu thị* (supermarket) or *siêu anh hùng* (superhero).

Think of it like saying “super” in English — more hype than neutral.

definitions.es: Un término informal utilizado para enfatizar que algo es muy bueno o impresionante.
definitions.en: An informal term used to emphasize something as being very good or impressive.
definitions.vi: Một thuật ngữ không chính thức được sử dụng để nhấn mạnh điều gì đó rất tốt hoặc ấn tượng.

exampleSentencesTranslated: The pho at this restaurant is super delicious, I want to eat more.
She just bought a super new and very beautiful phone.
I thought today’s test was super hard, I need to study more.
notes:
siêu = super, extremely. It makes something sound very impressive or intense.

It’s usually placed before adjectives to boost them, stronger and more emotional than rất.  

It’s informal and expressive, common in speech and online. In fixed words, it can also mean “super-” like in siêu thị (supermarket) or siêu anh hùng (superhero).

Think of it like saying “super” in English — more hype than neutral.
